The rotation happens at a specific node. The action `rotate` takes a node `?n`, a rotation `?r`, and the current heading `?fromdir` and the new heading `?todir`.
The `rotate` action sets `rotating` to true and starts the first pass.
The first pass updates headings and frees coordinates for nodes starting from the rotated node's neighbor.
The second pass sets the new coordinates.

Wait, looking at the `rotate` action:
`:parameters (?n - node ?r - rotation ?fromdir ?todir - direction)`
`:precondition (and (not (rotating)) (NEXT-DIRECTION ?fromdir ?r ?todir) (heading ?n ?fromdir))`
`:effect (and (not (heading ?n ?fromdir)) (heading ?n ?todir) (rotating) (node-first-pass-next ?n ?r ?n) ...)`

So `rotate` changes the heading of node `n`.
Then `node-first-pass-next` is set to `n`.
The first pass actions (`rotate-first-pass` and `rotate-first-pass-end`) propagate the change.
`rotate-first-pass` takes `?nstart`, `?r`, `?n1`, `?n2`, `?n2x`, `?n2y`, `?n2dir`, `?n2setdir`.
It updates `?n2`'s heading to `?n2setdir` (which is `?n2dir` rotated by `?r`).
It frees `?n2`'s old position.
It sets `node-first-pass-next` to `?n2`.
It continues until `END-NODE`.

The second pass actions (`rotate-second-pass` and `rotate-second-pass-end`) set the new coordinates.
`rotate-second-pass` takes `?n1`, `?n1x`, `?n1y`, `?n1dir`, `?n2`, `?n2x`, `?n2y`.
It checks if `?n2`'s position is free.
It sets `at ?n2 ?n2x ?n2y`.
It sets `node-second-pass-next` to `?n2`.
It continues until `END-NODE`.
